Analyst: Observation successful, conducted from a safety distance far greater than necessary. Effective nonetheless.
Zone analyzed from an anticipated fallback position. Excessive caution didn't hurt precision.
Reconnaissance complete despite three stops out of fear of being spotted. Result conforms.
Scouting done. I record: excess caution may have avoided unnecessary exposure.
Diplomat: The zone revealed itself to him... from far, very far away, but he still saw everything. That's already a lot.
Reconnaissance went smoothly, despite palpable fear at every movement. He held on.
I perceived what was needed, at a distance respectful of his own comfort. I understand that need.
Observation successful. His fear didn't stop him from completing his task, just from doing it differently.
Sentinel: Reconnaissance validated. Note: excessive operational distance observed, no impact on the final result.
Observation complete despite the operator's obvious nervousness throughout the mission.
Analysis successful from an unplanned but effective fallback position.
Scouting done. Report: operation successful despite high operational stress.
Explorer: I watched from super far away, like really far, but I still saw everything. That counts, right?
Okay I was terrified at the thought of getting closer, but the binoculars did the job.
I almost turned back a bunch of times. But I saw what was needed anyway.
Seriously I was scared, but I got the recon we needed. That's what matters.

Analyst: Reconnaissance failed. The excessive distance kept out of fear made the observation imprecise.
Observation impossible. I note a premature retreat that prevented any detailed analysis.
Analysis unsuccessful. Fear of exposure directly limited the quality of the data gathered.
Scouting failed. I record: three consecutive retreats in the face of minor risks, result compromised.
Diplomat: The zone didn't reveal itself... I think he didn't dare get close enough to really see.
Reconnaissance blocked. He hesitated too long, paralyzed by what could go wrong.
I perceived nothing. His fear, this time, won out over his necessary curiosity.
Observation impossible. I think he needed reassurance before getting any closer.
Sentinel: Reconnaissance failed. Logging: excessive operational distance that compromised the mission.
Analysis impossible. Report: the operator halted the approach following stress deemed excessive.
Observation unsuccessful after a hasty withdrawal by the operator in the face of limited risk.
Scouting not completed. I recommend psychological preparation before the next reconnaissance.
Explorer: I got too scared to get closer and saw nothing useful. Sorry.
Well, I panicked and stayed too far away to see anything.
I could have gotten closer, but I chickened out too much. Total failure as a result.
I saw a weird shadow and stopped everything. It was probably nothing, actually.
